{"id":"project-LICHEN-g7rs","title":"RouteCache.add does not check seq_num freshness","description":"File: python/src/lichen/loadng/cache.py
Lines: 55-60

The `add` method unconditionally replaces any existing entry:
```python
def add(self, entry: RouteEntry) -> None:
    self._entries[entry.destination] = entry  # Overwrites existing
    self._entries.move_to_end(entry.destination)
    while len(self._entries) > self.max_entries:
        self._entries.popitem(last=False)
```

Problem: There is no check for sequence number freshness. A stale route update (with an older seq_num) will overwrite a fresher route.

Per LOADng spec, when updating routes:
1. If new seq_num > existing seq_num: update (fresher info)
2. If new seq_num == existing seq_num and new metric < existing metric: update (better path)
3. Otherwise: ignore (stale or worse)

Impact:
1. Out-of-order updates can install stale routes
2. Delayed RREPs can degrade routing quality
3. Potential for route manipulation by injecting old seq_nums

Fix: Add freshness comparison:
```python
def add(self, entry: RouteEntry) -> None:
    existing = self._entries.get(entry.destination)
    if existing is not None:
        # Skip if existing is fresher
        if _is_seq_newer(existing.seq_num, entry.seq_num):
            return
        # Same seq_num: only update if better metric
        if existing.seq_num == entry.seq_num and entry.metric >= existing.metric:
            return
    self._entries[entry.destination] = entry
    ...
```","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","owner":"me@mark.atwood.name","created_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:22.510602Z","created_by":"Mark Atwood","updated_at":"2026-07-12T06:24:22.510634Z"}

Problem: There is no check for sequence number freshness. A stale route update (with an older seq_num) will overwrite a fresher route.

Per LOADng spec, when updating routes:
1. If new seq_num > existing seq_num: update (fresher info)
2. If new seq_num == existing seq_num and new metric < existing metric: update (better path)
3. Otherwise: ignore (stale or worse)

Impact:
1. Out-of-order updates can install stale routes
2. Delayed RREPs can degrade routing quality

```python
def add(self, entry: RouteEntry) -> None:
    existing = self._entries.get(entry.destination)
    if existing is not None:
        # Skip if existing is fresher

            return
        # Same seq_num: only update if better metric
        if existing.seq_num == entry.seq_num and entry.metric >= existing.metric:
            return
    self._entries[entry.destination] = entry
    ...
